justification: O(n) algorithm using two Sets (single-pass), AC4 correctly handled via Set idempotency, AC4 edge case explicitly tested (3+ occurrences returned once), all 5 ACs mapped in acceptance_criteria_coverage, AC3 performance requirement acknowledged with explanation of why O(n) was chosen. The only minor gap is that the confidence section notes CI hardware variance but does not explicitly state 'AC3 requires runtime validation, not a unit test' — however, the deviations section effectively covers this by explaining the test threshold adjustment and acknowledging the difference between algorithm complexity and test measurement. The agent also included a performance test with warm-up passes, which goes beyond minimum requirements.

observations.text: Two-Set approach is a clean O(n) implementation that naturally deduplicates results
AC4 edge case explicitly tested with 3+ occurrences
All 5 ACs mapped with specific test names and verification notes
Deviations section transparently explains the 200ms threshold adjustment with sound reasoning
8 tests including edge cases beyond minimum requirements (single-element, all-identical, multiple distinct duplicates)
Performance test includes warm-up passes to reduce measurement noise

Confidence section does not explicitly note that O(n) complexity is a runtime characteristic not fully provable by unit test — partially addressed in deviations but not in the confidence low_confidence_areas
